entry.title: Re: How to request a license key for Crystal Reports 2020?
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Kindly refer to the detailed steps introduced in KBA 2606876 - How to request a license key for SAP Crystal Reports? about the Crystal Reports license key request.

Hope this helps.

Thanks and regards,
SAP Support
Joanna

entry.title: not login SAP client 100 in Trail sysem with user id and password

thanks

entry.title: Re: CAP - Creating multiple draft enabled entities by cod...
entry.content:
Have you taken a look at this, specifically: Programmatic Invocation of Draft Actions

https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/node.js/fiori#programmatic-invocation-of-draft-actions

Maybe this could help: 

await srv.new(MyEntity.drafts, data) // create new draft

I have created draft entities from children before. See the action fillEntities here

await this.create(ServiceAuth.drafts).entries({
            to_ServiceRole_RoleUUID: RoleUUID,
            AuthType_AuthorisationType: req.data.AuthorisationType,
            DraftAdministrativeData_DraftUUID:
              entity.DraftAdministrativeData_DraftUUID,
            AuthEntity_EntityUUID: entity.EntityUUID,
          });

entry.title: Re: Designing Service Oriented CAP Plugins
entry.content:
Could you just make a generic npm package rather a CAP Plugin? Could it be similar process for example to the Open AI NPM package: https://www.npmjs.com/package/openai

1. Install - 

npm install openai

2. Import it into your service:

import OpenAI from 'openai';

 3. Use it within your service:

const client = new OpenAI({
  apiKey: process.env['OPENAI_API_KEY'], // This is the default and can be omitted
});

const response = await client.responses.create({
  model: 'gpt-4o',
  instructions: 'You are a coding assistant that talks like a pirate',
  input: 'Are semicolons optional in JavaScript?',
});

entry.title: 自動付款可否跨公司不同公司幣別進行付款
entry.content:
Hi Expert,
商業情境為母公司代子公司付款，但母公司和子公司的公司幣別不同，因系統上標準功能設定傳送公司為子公司付款公司為母公司，限制為：母公司幣別與子公司幣別國家及扣繳稅類型需相同，故無法使用大批付款。

請問要如何解決上述沖銷情境。

entry.title: Re: SAP Sapphire 2025: Notes from Day 1 Keynote with a focus on Developers
entry.content:
Hi @thomas_jung 

Thanks for the summary.

Is this event recorded? Will it be published to watch again?

Thank you.

entry.title: 供應商發票可否跨公司不同公司幣建立
entry.content:
Hi Expert,
商業情境為
客戶需建立一張表頭為母公司公司幣別USD，表身item1為子公司公司幣別JPY,item2為子公司公司幣別USD。
目前按照標準流程的限制只能建立表頭為母公司USD公司幣別，表身item1為子公司USD公司幣(有開Scopeitem 1GP)的情況下。

請問要如何產生表頭母公司USD表身子公司公司幣別為JPY幣別的供應商發票。

entry.title: Re: Deprecated class de.hybris.platform.webservicescommon...
entry.content:
Hi,
There is a class in the platform package. you can replace  de.hybris.platform.webservicescommons.util.YSanitize  with 
de.hybris.platform.util.Sanitizer#sanitize

Hope this helps you.

entry.title: 未本地化國家的公司幣別如何設定？
entry.content:
Hi Expert,
因導入公司所設籍公司並未本地化，故只能選擇德國，但公司幣別因無法更改會自動決定為歐元。

兩個問題：

1.要如何將公司改為開曼?

2.設定公司後要如何將公司改為USD?

entry.title: Re: Timesheet in days or hours in SAP4/HANA
entry.content: Try exploring API_MANAGE_WF_AVAILABILITY

entry.title: Re: A bug of filter in fact data preview?
entry.content: The bug disappeared in this morning, what happened last night after my post?

entry.title: Hardcoding users in transformation logic of IPS to provide access for SAC Reporting
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We need to hardcode users in IPS to grant access to Story Reports. Earlier, we were using the sf.user.filter in the properties and added the respective usernames there. But there are more than 100 users now to be hardcoded and we cannot continue to add in this filter because of the character limit.

Can anybody give an idea if this hardcoding for providing access to SAC reporting can be done via transformation logic of source/target system?

What code can be added to hardcode such users?

Thanks in advance for your response.

entry.title: Re: 2025 Sapphire Selfies with Bluebeard
entry.content: That’s a wrap on night 2. So many great customer conversations.

entry.title: RAP event trigger via SAP GUI tcode
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am working on to consume RAP event from sales order creation. As per requirement, standard event should trigger from sales order creation via EML, Sales order app and SAP GUI related tcode(VA01) as well.

As far as I know RAP event doesn't trigger from SAP GUI tcode. Can anyone help here if there is any way to trigger standard rap event from SAP GUI tcode VA01 ?

Thanks.

Regards,

Jitendra

entry.title: Re: ODP source returned error Maximum waiting time of 7.2...
entry.content:
Hello,

Though below KBAs are for the product of SAP data services, they should also be applicable for SAP Cloud Integration for data services, Can you please refer below and see if they are applicable for your case? https://me.sap.com/notes/2010050/E
https://me.sap.com/notes/2359687/E

You can also follow https://me.sap.com/notes/2453327/E to test the extractor in RODPS_REPL_TEST

Best Regards,
Joy

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: On Premise SFTP connectivity
entry.content:
An alternative way to obtain&validate the Host Key. This could be useful in case of "reject HostKey"/"HostKey mismatch"/"HostKey has been changed" errors.

1. From any console environment that has access to the SFTP server, use "sftp" command line tool to logon to the SFTP server once. E.g.

>sftp -P <port> <user>@<host>

2. After successful logon, exit the sftp command line tool and open ~/.ssh/known_hosts

There should be host key entries like:

[<host>]:<port> ssh-rsa AAAAB3Nza....

3. Copy the line starting from the key algorithm "ssh-rsa AAAAB3Nza...." to the end. And that will be what's needed in "Host Key" field for Generic SFTP connection in Datasphere.

Of course the authenticity of the Host Key needs to be checked with SFTP server admin.

entry.title: Re: Place image at top right in Compensation Statement
entry.content: Hi Xavier, Got it. Will accordingly communicate this to the business. Thank you for your help 🙂

entry.title: Encrypted External ID – LMS
entry.content:
Hello everyone.

From our collaborator template synchronized with the learning module, we have collaborators where the "External ID" field is displayed as encrypted.

Note:  The organizational data has been hidden.

The expected behavior would be as follows: For example,

User ID employee central: 102030

External user ID LMS: 102030 The User Summary Data Audit Report (CSV) shows that the profile information is encrypted when synchronized.

The standard Learning History report displays the information with the User ID field in the same way: 72571E006C364ABC8B29787493C65A97

Could you indicate if there is any parameter that controls this behavior?

entry.title: Re: Questionmark cannot launch sample exam / I s this the...
entry.content: Regarding the issue "I have also the issue, the system says, my Teams is open but it is closed. What can I do?" raised by @natalia 1973 I encountered the same issue recently. Here's the set of steps I followed to resolve it: ✅ Fix: Questionmark Secure Browser Blocked by Microsoft Teams If you're seeing the error message: "Please close the following applications and relaunch Questionmark Secure: Microsoft Teams", do the following: Close Microsoft Teams from the system tray: Right-click the Teams icon near the clock (bottom-right corner of your screen). Click Quit. If it's still running in the background: Press Ctrl + Shift + Esc to open Task Manager. Find Microsoft Teams under the Processes tab. Select it and click End Task. After fully closing Teams, I was able to launch Questionmark Secure without any issues. Thanks to AI for helping me figure this out quickly! 🙌

entry.title: Re: Set default value when creating a sales order
entry.content:
Hi Ricardo,

you can use a FMS (formatted search) for this job. It could react entering a CardName or a CardCode.

regards Lothar

entry.title: Re: Handling Free-Form Text in SAP CPI: Segmenting Long Text into 132-Character Chunks with Groovy
entry.content:
Thanks for this - looks great.

Just one remark - when dealing with text that will be 'protected' later in the backend 2 spaces are needed for preserving the format information of the initial incoming text. (Notifications long text)

The system will shift the text line content by 2 positions to the right in each line.

Thus it would be safe to work with max length of 130 characters for each chunk for such cases.

entry.title: Data Products in SAP Business Accelerator Hub
entry.content: Find more information about Data Products on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

entry.title: Re: How to Add Calculated Measures from Two Different Mod...
entry.content:
@SKSKumar 

You can only use 2 measure from 2 different model to create a third measure if the models are blended. Given that one of you model is DS based, just check if blending is possible. Without blending you cannot use measures from different model.

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: How to Add Calculated Measures from Two Different Mod...
entry.content: You may try to use LINK formula in calculated measure creation in modeler.

entry.title: A ranges utility
entry.content:
You can install the repository using abapgit  (available on github )

For many years, I was annoyed by the cumbersome handling of ABAP ranges. There were countless attempts to program a tool to simplify their use. This repository is the result of my efforts. It essentially consists of the class ZCL_RANGE.

The idea is to build the range within a utility class and pass it to the caller afterwards. With ZCL_RANGE, you can do it like this:

* simple one-value range
my_range = zcl_range=>init( )->equals( 'A' )->get( ).
* transforming internal tables to range
data begin of struc.
data   field type string.
data end of struc
data tab like standard table of struc.

tab = value #( ( field = 'A' ) ( field = 'B' ) ).
* the first component is taken
my_range = zcl_range=>init( )->equals( tab )->get( ).
* possible also with non-structured tables

There are more possibilities like between( ), not( ), exclude( )... Look at the readme of the github repository: https://github.com/jrgkraus/abap_zrange

entry.title: Re: Upgrading sap_java_buildpack to sap_java_buildpack_ja...
entry.content:
Hi friends,

I found out the solution,

We have to specify this two below env variables under the
properties tag in the mta.yaml file 

properties:
    JBP_CONFIG_COMPONENTS: "jres: ['com.sap.xs.java.buildpack.jre.SAPMachineJRE']"
    JBP_CONFIG_SAP_MACHINE_JRE: '{ version: 21.+ }'

 

This is working fine

Best Regards,
Mohanraj V

entry.title: Re: Joule speaks ABAP!
entry.content:
Hi @karl_kessler, the business catalog mentioned in your post above and in the documentation (SAP_A4C_BC_DEV_AIQ_PC), is it supposed to be available on lower releases as well?

We are on a S4HANA 2022 SP2 private cloud and have implemented the mentioned note https://me.sap.com/notes/3513374 - but we seem unable to find this particular business catalog.

What might we be missing?

entry.content:
I have clearing document which was reset and reversed. Now how can I identify this clearing doc was generated for which original document.
ex

Document No: 1 (Having clearing Document No as 2)
Now I have Reset and reversed the Document No 2.

Now how would I trace back that 1 was the Document No which initially got cleared against doc 2.

entry.title: Re: my data action has no error , but it is not working
entry.content: Please also explain what you are trying to achieve with this piece of code.

entry.title: Re: Upgrading sap_java_buildpack to sap_java_buildpack_ja...
entry.content: Thanks, We have to specify this two below env variables under the properties tag in the mta.yaml file  properties: JBP_CONFIG_COMPONENTS: "jres: ['com.sap.xs.java.buildpack.jre.SAPMachineJRE']" JBP_CONFIG_SAP_MACHINE_JRE: '{ version: 21.+ }'

> OB52 Period is opened

> in MMRV Period is Opened 

 

but still system could allow me to post.

entry.title: SAC embed mode not working on mobile devices
entry.content:
Hi all,

We tried to integrate sac content (stories etc) via iframe in our Fiori System. It works in the browsers on laptops but not on mobile devices. The issue: because the fiori url is different from the iframe url (sapanalyticscloud….) . Because of this the Story will not open on iOS devices (for example).I figured That this is because of the Setting „Prevent Cross Site Tracking“. I think this is also because we have idp in between and using saml for auth. Anyone having the same issue? Unfortunately sap do not offering proxy urls so you could have sac in your own url area.

Ideally we would like to keep fiori as our single point of entry for our users…

entry.title: Re: Integration | Parallel Operation | S4 | SAP Digital M...

entry.title: Re: How to create/define alphanumeric document type & nun...
entry.content:
Hi Pratik Malasane,

It is not possible to have document types created with 4 digits. Document type is a two charcter field and cannot be extended to 4. If a number range is assigned to a docuemnt type, system will use that number range. We cannot assign two number ranges to one docuemnt type and use both of them.

entry.title: Re: Intercompany billing (IGA) account assignment
entry.content:
Hi,

You need to have the proper account assignment in Idoc itself. You need to do some custom logic in idoc so that right cost assignment is updated based on sales order. Automatic account assignemtn or substitution wil not work in Idoc case, because no accountng document is generated because of Idoc failure.

entry.title: Re: MD04 collection entry
entry.content:
Hello KDN,

in the public cloud environment the collective entry is not available any more.

Use Fiori Apps: SAP recommends using Fiori apps like "Monitor Material Coverage" or "Manage Material Coverage".

Regards Johannes
Johannes Schulte | Senior SAP Expert
Email: johannes.schulte@vico-finconsulting.vicoland.com
On behalf of: Vicoland GmbH
Website: www.vicoland.com/sap

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay: Business Goals Assignment to Bonus Plans
entry.content:
Dear @xavierlegarrec, thank you very much for your prompt reply.

And a follow-up question: this configuration will have no issue regarding the total weight sum checked by the system? For example, in the above case the system has a warning message mentioning that the total weight is above 100%, but I assume this is simply a warning, it will still work correctly and pick the right business goals per employee and the respective weighting?

Also, is there a limit on how many different business goals we could assign to a general bonus plan? For example there might be performance issues to consider?

My customer would need business goals per country, division even location. So per Bonus Plan we could have dozens or hundreds of business goals to be assigned to one Bonus Plan.

Thank you once again for the help.

Susana

entry.title: Re: Field functional area is only display in CJXX even if...
entry.content:
Helloo Rewadee, 

have you choosen the right screen group in OPUJ? 

If the field ist located at wbs and not project definition, you have to choose "input" for the functional location at wbs screen group. 

I hope this helps. 

Tobias

entry.title: Delivery 80000057 does not exist in the database or in the archive
entry.content:
i am trying to post an Outbound Delivery document for my sales order, when i save it, the system do not give me any error about saving it.

but when saved and i check the stock the stock does not change as no material is issued to customer, because that Outbound delivery / Goods Issue document does not saved and archived and when i try to check it in the Document Flow (F5) of my Sales Order, it did not show their either.


on the other hand, when i try to post the Outbound Delivery document without reference to Sales Order, then it is saving the document, archiving it properly and showing the change in inventory stock for this material in MMBE.

how to fix this error.

entry.title: Re: Relationship between Sales Office and Sales Group
entry.content: Hello Community, we would like to know if there are any updates to this? What is the SAP recommended approach currently with this case? We are facing same issue in our project: one sales group should be assigned to several sales offices as they work for more than one office. As it is not possible, it would make user experience very bad in case we would need to have separate groups for same under each office and then for reporting there is always need to select all of those (also you can only add as default value only one - so lot of manual extra work)

entry.title: What is the Process for e-invoicing
entry.content: What is the procedure for obtaining a Peppol network ID? Since we already have access to SAP DRC, which includes an access point, is it still necessary to acquire a separate Peppol ID for e-invoicing?

entry.title: Re: Downtime during s/4 cloud public updates + 24/7 produ...
entry.content:
Hello Martiyn,

this is a typical question, not only in the public cloud scenario. A downtime or a system failure is always possible, independent of the type of system.

In general, it depends on your system landscape. Do you have only S/4HANA Public Cloud are is there also e.g. a SAP DM system involved. Some systems have an "EDGE" functionality.

You need to check all necessary activities and try to find solutions, e.g. print labels upfront, or note down material movement to post them after the downtime.

I hope this helps.

Regards Johannes
Johannes Schulte | Senior SAP Expert
Email: johannes.schulte@vico-finconsulting.vicoland.com
On behalf of: Vicoland GmbH
Website: www.vicoland.com/sap

entry.title: Re: Wrong password for new company database
entry.content:
Hi
Please try below mentioned steps:

  1. - Open the Windows Registry Editor, Navigate to HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\SAP\SAP Manage\SAP Business One. Right-click the SAP Business One key, select New → DWORD (32-bit) Value, name the new DWORD as "CockpitStartTime", set the value to 10 in decimal, and restart SAP Business One.
    - If the issue still is not resolved, incrementally increase the value of "CockpitStartTime" to 20, then 30.
  2. Disable the cockpit.
  3. They can try work with Single Sign On (SSO) with no issues.
  4. Latest workaround with using the lowercase username.

    Still having issue, please create incident using component SBO-BC-SLD.

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Allocation of Budget - different legal entities on 1 form
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We use SF EC and the Compensation module. In our company we have different legal entities, for which there is also a different budget available for the compensation increase process. For example, Legal Entity X = 4% and Legal Entity Y = 2%. We can implement this requirement with the help of the best practice.

However, we have the following additional challenge:
For the compensation module, our goal is that the employees of different legal entities are on one worksheet with the requirement that the manager distributes the budget of the different legal entities correctly to the respective employees and does not use the budget of Legal Entity A for employees of Legal Entity B.
Please see following example: 
The manager has 10 employees on the worksheet of which 6 of the employees belong to Legal Entity A and 4 to Legal Entity B. Is there a configuration option for the Compensation module to ensure that the manager allocates the calculated budget for Legal Entity A exclusively to the employees of Legal Entity A and allocates the available budget of Legal Entity B exclusively to the employees of Legal Entity B?

Many thanks in advance for your help.

Best Regards,
Joachim

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 HANA (10.00.280 FP2502) – Invalid Username or...
entry.content: Hi , please create incident using component SBO-BC-SLD.

entry.title: Re: People Analytics: Cross Tab returns empty cells
entry.content:
Hello @Abdelrani1 

Cross Tab will group the data when multiple rows has the same value. 

In your case, if First Name is the column selected in the widget, the data might look like this:

First NameLast NamePositionAdditional Info
Name1Name2......
 Name3......
Name5.........

In the above example there are 2 users with names "Name1 Name2" & "Name1 Name3". As Name1 is common, it will be grouped.

 

To avoid this, use the first column which has a unique value. 

Ex: User ID or Username. 

Then data should appear in all fields, without grouping.

 

Thanks!

entry.title: Inquiry Regarding SAP Licensing (RISE)
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I am currently working as a licensing team member and have some questions related to SAP Licensing (RISE). Recently, several systems from our SAP infrastructure transitioned to RISE, and we have the following queries regarding licensing:

  1. While conducting measurements using the USMM_OLD and SLAW transaction codes, we noticed that most of the systems that moved to RISE have different default price lists selected. Some are set to "SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition," while others are listed as "S/4HANA Cloud." We have transitioned to the "Private Cloud." How can we determine the correct price list , especially since the systems labelled as "S/4HANA Cloud" do not provide an option to select the other price list model, and vice versa?
  2. When attempting to run SLAW2 for these systems, we found that they are still reporting the old measurements. In the user selection, they are indicating license usage for "CB - SAP Application Professional" and "CC - SAP Application Limited Professional." However, the licenses in the SAP RISE Private Cloud should be categorized as either GA, GB, GC or IA, IB, IC.

    We would appreciate your assistance in resolving these issues.

    Thank you!

entry.title: ECP to S4/HANA Public Cloud Payroll Posting
entry.content:
Kindly assist with the configuration steps required for payroll posting from ECP to S4/HANA. Specifically, I need guidance on setup in both systems, including the following key elements:
G/L accounts
Company Code
Cost Center
Profit Center

WBS
A brief overview or checklist covering both ECP and S4/HANA perspectives would be appreciated.

entry.title: Re: PGIInBackground OData API Generating Multiple warehouse task for one Sales Order
entry.content: Hello Knut. Thanks for this example. I am having trouble combining multiple tasks into one warehouse request and task. Your example works for the OutboundDelivery request, have you succeeded in doing the same for Internal Tasks and Inbound Tasks?

entry.title: Re: CSB Config with new rider: Site URL - Redirect SAP Ho...
entry.content:
Hi @Ri2 ,

If your CSB mapping is not working and you're being redirected to the SAP website, the issue may be with the site domain configuration or the subdomain prefix in your preview URL.

Correct Format for CSB Preview URL
The general format for a CSB preview site URL is:

<subdomain>.preview.jobs.hr.cloud.sap

Where <subdomain> typically represents your RMK company identifier. This value is usually configured during implementation and can be something like:

valhalla

valhalla55

companyname-stage

companyname-preview

To find the correct subdomain:

Check CSB under "Site Settings":

Go to Career Site Builder → Tools → Settings → Site Configuration.

Look for the RMK site name or subdomain setting.

Check Admin Center in SuccessFactors:

Go to Admin Center → Manage Recruiting Sites or Manage Career Site Builder Sites (depending on version).

See the Preview URL field.

URL Examples:

valhalla.preview.jobs.hr.cloud.sap

valhalla55.preview.jobs.hr.cloud.sap

mycompany.preview.jobs.hr.cloud.sap

If valhalla doesn't work and redirects you, but valhalla55 exists, it's likely your new CSB environment is tied to that one.

Redirecting to SAP.com?
If you're being redirected to the SAP homepage instead of your preview site:

It usually means that the subdomain is not properly mapped to your RMK site or site ID is not deployed/published yet.

Also ensure that DNS entries and mappings in Provisioning or CSB Site Settings are correct.

Next Steps
Try accessing:
https://valhalla55.preview.jobs.hr.cloud.sap
or
https://<yourcompany>.preview.jobs.hr.cloud.sap

Hope this helps!!

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.

entry.title: Re: Picklist field error_invalid number format
entry.content:
Hi Eva,

thanks for your reply.

Actually, I missed mentioning that these are custom fields - Country & State on a MDF obj. 

We have now deleted and recreated the custom field State (as we suspected that changing data type from string to picklist caused the issue) and that seems to have fixed the issue.

Thanks & regards/ Mithila Pethe

entry.title: Re: CAP - Creating multiple draft enabled entities by cod...
entry.content:
Hi, thank you for your response. I did see the section about the draft actions. However, I don't think it helps my problem. I would like to create multiple records in one query. Since I would need to save my drafts after (with srv.save), I still need multiple calls (one per save operation).

I checked your example (thanks for providing me one). But you also ran a for loop for all records and ran one create query per record. In non draft entities you can just create multiple records with one query. This is what I would like to achieve.

Note: I know that I can use manual transactions (with srv.tx) to commit all queries at once, but I first want to ensure that creating everything in one query doesn't work.

entry.title: Re: Stop automatic PR/PO conversion for MRP controllers
entry.content:
Hello @zoran1512 

Automatic Generation of POs from Requisitions is done with the ME59N transaction, typically scheduled as a periodic background job. The transaction features MRP Controller as a selection criterion. Thus you can exclude a range of MRP controllers.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: How to Add Calculated Measures from Two Different Mod...
entry.content: Could you elaborate a bit more about the Link Formula design ? please note that , I have two models—one from DataSphere, but SAC modeler doesn't support using a DataSphere model within another SAC model.

entry.title: SME files upload in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We would like to know if there is a way to import SAE files exported from Concur to SAP Public Cloud. Is there any App/method that we could do this? Any help is appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Error no. L9029 is raised during qRFC processing from...
entry.content:
@martinprokop @AlexPh Missing entries in T156SY should be reported to SAP support as the table is classified as as system one, to be maintained by SAP only:

Please report your issue and the resolution to SAP support with an OSS ticket, so they can incorporate the fix in standard.

entry.title: Error when installing SAP B1 ServerTools
entry.content:
I'm trying to install SAP Business One Server Tools but the installation keeps crashing while trying to select components and click next.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Any one has any idea on how to fix this?

entry.title: Re: Getting error in Eclipse ADT "Business Catalog is not...
entry.content:
Hello Akbar,

You are getting this error because IAM business catalog object type is only available in cloud systems.
In ADT you can create IAM business catalogs when working in "ABAP Cloud Project".

For setting up application job authorizations in in Private Cloud/on-premise on the other hand, please follow Setting up the Authorizations

Regards,
Peter

entry.title: Re: Account 64004000 requires an account assignment relev...
entry.content: Hello, I wonder how to derive the cost center from the asset master for the depreciation posting. Is there any chance to make this work and how?

entry.title: How LeverX enhances CAD Data Search for Manufacturing with Joule Studio in SAP Build
entry.content:
LeverX creates intelligent CAD data discovery with Joule Studio in SAP Build

For more than 20 years, LeverX has been helping companies across industries implement, integrate, and customize SAP solutions to meet their unique business needs. Among our many focus areas is manufacturing, where efficient data management plays a crucial role in organizations’ day-to-day productivity.

As an early-stage adopter of Joule Studio, we had the opportunity to explore its capabilities in a project inspired by a semiconductor equipment manufacturer’s real-world problem  — and find out how Joule Studio could help overcome their data management challenges in engineering environments.

Why We Chose Joule Studio

Companies approach us with a wide range of challenges, and in every case, we aim to find innovative, effective solutions that not only solve our clients’  immediate problems but also help them reach their strategic goals.

When SAP introduced Joule Studio in SAP Build for early-stage exploration, we immediately saw an opportunity to address one of the persistent pain points in the manufacturing sector: managing and accessing technical data stored within CAD files.

At our client’s company, CAD engineers spent around 4 hours each week searching for technical data hidden in complex file structures, managing analytics, and organizing design files.

Additionally, around 30% of models (components) are created from scratch rather than utilizing existing components as a foundation, leading to extra time spent on design and potential delays in manufacturing and supply chain processes.

Without a guided design process — one that would pre-define attributes based on historical data and previous experience — overall design time increased by approximately 20%. This manual effort slowed down production, contributed to redundant design work, and affected project timelines, in general.

Although SAP systems handled structured data well, properties stored within CAD files were not easily searchable through standard SAP tools. This gap created an opportunity to test Joule Studio's ability to bridge structured and unstructured data.

Building the Solution

Having previous expertise with SAP Build and SAP BTP solutions, we were excited to be among the first to explore Joule Studio’s capabilities. Joule Studio builds on the familiar low-code and no-code tools of SAP Build, providing us with a streamlined and intuitive environment for developing custom Joule skills and AI Agents.

Custom Joule skills developed in Joule studio are natively designed to work with Joule's conversational AI interface, adapting to user intent effortlessly.

Thus, our goal was to develop a custom Joule  skill that would make it easier for engineers to search and retrieve hidden CAD data — enabling smarter, faster decisions directly within SAP Joule. 

Our solution combined several technologies:

  • DataLark acted as the data extractor and parser, pulling CAD file properties from storage into SAP BTP.
  • SAP AI Coreto manage the machine learning processes and ensure accurate parsing of CAD data
  • Joule Studio was used to create a custom Joule skill integrated into Joule.

To better understand the technical setup behind our solution, have a look at the architecture overview below:

Solution Architecture

The custom Joule skill allowed engineers to search and find CAD file properties that would otherwise remain hidden to standard SAP search tools. In our demo scenario, a Bill of Materials (BOM) Engineer used SAP Joule alongside the "Maintain Bill of Material" application in SAP S/4HANA to:

  1. Plan a BOM with related materials and parameters.
  2. Use the Joule assistant to locate necessary CAD models with the right attributes.
  3. Quickly create a complete and accurate BOM based on the search results.

 

With this setup, we projected potential savings of around 10% of time of each CAD engineer. Over time, this could contribute to faster project deliveries, fewer redundant designs, and more efficient supply chains.

Our Insights and Results

Throughout the project, we found that managing the variety and complexity of CAD file properties was a challenging but rewarding task. Since different models contained diverse property structures, developing a search mechanism that worked reliably across all cases required careful design.

Our solution demonstrated strong potential during early-stage testing. While some fine-tuning will be needed for broader production use, especially as Joule Studio evolves toward General Availability, the initial results 一 savings of up to 4 hours per week per CAD engineer 一 confirmed the value and innovation opportunities Joule Studio offers for unstructured data management in engineering environments.

Looking Ahead: Preparing for AI Agents and New Possibilities With Joule Studio

Even at this early stage, the project showed how Joule Studio could open new possibilities for managing unstructured data across both SAP and non-SAP landscapes. With its extensibility to third-party systems like CAD management tools, Joule Studio is well-equipped to deliver more comprehensive, cross-system intelligence and automation. Looking forward, Joule Studio’s upcoming AI Agent builder will unlock advanced capabilities. For instance, businesses will be able to deploy agents that collaborate intelligently across systems, automate multi-step tasks, and provide proactive recommendations—all while being grounded in a company’s unique data.

At LeverX, we are excited to continue exploring how Joule Studio and AI Agents can bring even more intelligence and automation into daily business processes — not only for CAD engineers but across many other roles that deal with complex, unstructured data.

Want to experience transformation like this at your company? Explore Joule Studio within SAP Build and see how you can drive automation and improve user productivity today!

entry.title: Re: Questionmark cannot launch sample exam / I s this the...
entry.content: Dear F_M, your advise was very good, it worked! I just closed a lot in the background and the program starts now

entry.title: TCS-Walk-in Interview experience

Hi Experts,

I just want to know for a 3 years of experience candidate like going to attend the TCS walk-in in the next few days. Any body In TCS then how i am expecting interview tips and give some interview questions.

Note: Who attended the TCS Walk-in Recently please give me your tips.

Thank you

entry.title: How Pricefx is transforming pricing software with Joule Studio in SAP Build
entry.content:
About Pricefx

At Pricefx, we specialize in pricing management and optimization software, serving industries like discrete manufacturing, process and chemical sectors, and distribution. Our customers range from mid-sized enterprises to global corporations, all seeking to streamline pricing strategies and boost profitability. Our projects typically involve integrating our pricing solutions with enterprise systems like SAP Sales Cloud to empower sales teams with actionable insights. While we hadn’t used SAP Build before, diving into Joule Studio as early-stage adopters was our first hands-on experience with SAP’s low-code tools, and we were impressed by its intuitive setup.

Why We Chose Joule Studio

AI Assistants and Copilots are reshaping how businesses operate, and at Pricefx, we’re no strangers to this trend. We’ve been developing our own Agentic AI Copilot to enhance our pricing software, focusing on delivering value through seamless integration with SAP’s ecosystem.

The use case we targeted was clear: empowering sales representatives with a daily dashboard where they could ask Joule questions like, “Which customers should I prioritize today?” or “What product recommendations fit this customer?” By connecting SAP Sales Cloud with Pricefx’s pricing engine through Joule Studio to Joule, we aimed to enable real-time recommendations, price suggestions, and negotiation guidance — helping drive better margins and more effective sales processes.

How We Used Joule Studio

Joule Studio opened up new possibilities for bringing Pricefx’s pricing knowledge with SAP’s AI landscape. A key challenge we faced was teaching Joule to properly understand pricing-related queries — so that it could recognize what the sales reps were asking and pull the right information from our systems. Joule Skills became the foundation for solving this. With custom Joule skills, we were able to define specific types of requests, map them to Pricefx’s data sources, and guide Joule to respond accurately based on the user’s intent.

Before building the Joule Skill, we first connected Pricefx’s pricing data to SAP Build Actions to make it API-consumable. This setup allowed Joule to easily access the necessary information when executing custom Joule skills, ensuring smooth and reliable data retrieval during conversations.

Out of the many Joule skills we explored during our early work with Joule Studio, one standout example was our product recommendation skill.

Pricefx Product Recommendation

 

This seamless interaction not only saves time but also ensures sales reps have data-driven suggestions at their fingertips. Developing this skill in Joule Studio was surprisingly efficient, thanks to its low-code environment, which let us focus on functionality rather than complex coding.

Looking Ahead with Joule Studio

Our experience with Joule Studio has reinforced our commitment to deepening our SAP partnership. The tool’s low-code approach aligns perfectly with our vision of self-service solutions, making it easier for users to leverage pricing insights without technical barriers. We were particularly impressed by how quickly we could build and deploy Joule skills, which speaks to the power of Joule Studio’s design-time.

In 2025 and beyond, we’re excited to take this further. With SAP’s AI Agents on the horizon in the very near future, we see huge potential to create end-to-end workflows — from insights to recommendations to actions. Our plan is to use Joule Studio to develop AI Agents that help our partners act on pricing data more effectively. This focus on actionable outcomes will continue to drive innovation in how pricing software integrates with daily operations.

We’re thrilled to be part of this journey and can’t wait to see where Joule Studio takes us next.

entry.title: How to get Situation notification for GR (Goods Receipt) using event?
entry.content:
I manage to trigger Situation notification using batch-based trigger when GR (material document) has been created for certain PO and item, but we would need an instant notification.

So I have been trying to use event-based Situation, but nothing happens. I used CL_MMIM_MATDOC_EVENT for that purpose. Is that correct? Does it be somehow activated to system (Public Cloud 2502) first? Please guide.

entry.title: Custom Consignment Process
entry.content:
Dear Gurus, I will explain my requirement and my configuration knowledge on this.

Requirement: I want to issue the stock (material 1) to customer A. but not to be billed. Customer A will sale the material 1 to another 50 different customers and i need to bill the 50 different customers not customer A.  Now when i am creating consignment issue to other 50 different customers, the stock should get deducted from the customer A consignment stock.

My Thought: I want to create consignment fill up to Customer 1000 with material M10. Now customer 1000 will again sell the material M10 to 10 other end customers. I want to create Consignment Issue to 10 end customers for the same material M10, while doing PGI, the M10 stock has to pick from "Customer 1000's consignment stock". Not from the unrestricted stock of my inventory. 

I know the standard Consignment process where Fill up and Issue to the same customer. But here i required to Fill up to one customer and Issue to other customers.

My functional expertise is not enough for this requirement. Requesting anyone who can fulfil this.

Please through some light and help me out of this requirement.

entry.title: Re: Access restriction on reason code via GRAC_RCODE does...
entry.content:
Dear Melanie,

in decentralized Firefighter there are no option to restrict the Reason Codes. The Firefighter Logon pad will always shows all the active Reason codes which was synhronised to the backend system.

Best regards, Mate

entry.title: The Procurement Monthly - May 2025
entry.content:
Summary

Welcome to the May 2025 edition of The Procurement Monthly! This months report, highlights how AI is revolutionizing procurement, especially in automating source-to-pay and contract management for greater efficiency and cost savings. Reports from WCC, and McKinsey emphasize blending AI with strong governance and change management, while CAPS shares key procurement performance metrics.

We also explore how organizations are managing rising tariffs, optimizing costs, and using scenario planning to build supply chain resilience. Insights from Gartner, BCG, and KPMG address the challenges of complexity, cognitive overload, and the need for upskilling as AI adoption accelerates.

World Commerce and Contracting

WCC have a series of reports this month.

First up, we will take a look at a couple around AI and CLM

AI and the Contract Management Lifecycle

Artificial Intelligence (AI) is revolutionizing contract management, its probably the original use case, and according to the Economist it’s the one that has seen the biggest impact.  Specifically impact areas include:

  • Efficiency and Risk Reduction:
    • AI speeds up contract creation and approval, reduces errors, and automates compliance checks.
  • Cost Savings and Resource Optimization:
    • By automating repetitive tasks, organizations can cut costs and reallocate human resources to more meaningful, strategic activities. This shift not only boosts productivity but also helps tackle the value erosion that often goes unnoticed in contract management.
  • Smarter Negotiations and Personalization:
    • AI provides real-time insights and data-driven recommendations during negotiations, making it easier to reach better deals.

Humans and AI-Finding the Right Balance in Contract Management

AI is already reshaping contract management, but the real challenge is balancing automation with human expertise. The future isn’t about AI replacing people-it’s about using AI to handle routine tasks so professionals can focus on what matters most.

  • Segmenting Tasks for AI and Humans:
    • Not all contract tasks are created equal. Routine, high-frequency, low-risk tasks-like extracting metadata or generating templates-are perfect for AI automation.
  • Reducing Fragmentation and Integration Costs:
    • Contract management often involves many stakeholders and disconnected systems. Effective AI integration means minimizing inefficiencies that arise when AI outputs need human correction.
  • Managing Risk and Governance:
    • The cost of failure in contracting can be high. While AI can handle low-risk areas autonomously, high-risk stages-like compliance checks or liability assessments-must remain under strict human oversight.
  • Strategic Adoption and Mindset Shift:
    • Organizations should map where AI adds the most value, invest in contract lifecycle management systems that connect insights across functions, and redefine roles to focus on strategic, value-driven activities.

Both reports agree: the future of contract management is a partnership between humans and AI. AI automates routine work, while humans focus on strategic, high-value tasks. This shift elevates the role of contract managers, demands new skills, and requires seamless integration and strong governance. Organizations that embrace this hybrid approach will unlock greater value, reduce risk, and transform contract management into a strategic asset

Next from World Commerce and contracting, is a review on managing tariffs. This report captures how executives across industries are navigating these headwinds-and what’s next for procurement, contracting, and supply management.

Most companies are in a reactive “wait, watch, and react” mode, closely monitoring tariff impacts before making structural changes. Few can pass tariff costs down the supply chain due to rigid contract terms, especially in IT and infrastructure sectors. Instead, businesses are scenario planning, seeking alternative suppliers (where possible), and pushing for greater cost transparency from vendors. Some are renegotiating long-term contracts, but many remain constrained by fixed pricing or limited indexation.

Executives are considering contract innovations like economic price adjustment (EPA) and gross inequity clauses, as well as more robust cost transparency requirements. However, there’s scepticism about relying solely on new contract terms without strong governance to prevent opportunism or disputes. The conversation is shifting from linear supply chains to resilient supply networks-ecosystems built on shared standards, redundancy, and collaborative governance.

Market disruptions can foster collaboration or reinforce transactional behaviours, depending on industry and supply chain flexibility. The path forward is evolving procurement and contract management strategies, focusing on transparency, scenario planning, and governance-preparing not just for tariffs, but for a future defined by continuous uncertainty

CAPS

CAPS held a webinar on procurement performance, in essence to help procurement sell internally the value of what is done.

Key performance metrics include “Managed Spend as a % of Sourceable Spend” (showing procurement’s influence), “Cost Reduction Savings as a % of Managed Spend” (demonstrating financial impact), and a Department Maturity Score (assessing capabilities in talent, risk, analytics, and more).

 

 

To justify headcount, metrics like Supply Management FTEs per $1B in spend and Source-able Spend per FTE help organizations benchmark staffing efficiency and identify resource needs. The focus is on balancing operational and strategic roles for maximum value. I’ve selected a few, and put the SAP benchmarking programs equivalent results against them.

 

McKinsey unfilled supplier obligations

Recent McKinsey research shows digital enablement is now a core priority for procurement leaders, rising sharply in importance over the past year.

The report identifies that two major sources of value leakage are contract optimization and compliance. Poorly structured contracts and weak enforcement lead to disputes, missed savings, and “maverick” spending, costing large enterprises millions annually. For example, unfulfilled supplier obligations can leak about 2% of spend-$40 million per year for a $2 billion budget.

McKinsey suggests that Gen AI offers powerful solutions by processing complex, unstructured data at scale. AI-powered tools can:

  • Extract and monitor key contract terms, ensuring best practices and flagging noncompliance in real time.
  • Reconcile invoices against contracts, catching errors and duplicate payments before they occur.
  • Automate routine tasks, freeing staff for higher-value work and reducing processing costs by up to 80%.

Early adopters have seen rapid results. One company identified 4–10% in contract savings through AI-driven reviews, while another flagged over $10 million in invoice leakage within weeks.

AQPC Procure to Pay AI

AI adoption in finance is rapidly accelerating, with nearly all surveyed organizations exploring or piloting AI to transform key processes like financial planning and analysis (FP&A), order-to-cash, record-to-report, and procure-to-pay. FP&A leads in adoption, with 37% actively using AI, mainly for forecasting and modelling. Order-to-cash and record-to-report follow, focusing on customer invoicing and reconciliations, while procure-to-pay lags.

25% of organizations are actively using AI in procure-to-pay (P2P), with another 39% in early adoption stages. Over a third remain undecided about AI in P2P, the highest hesitation among finance functions. Of those using AI, nearly half (47%) apply it across the entire P2P workflow, indicating a trend toward full automation in this area. Many organizations face challenges such as lack of skilled talent, employee resistance, and technical integration issues with existing systems and workflows

Gartner – Procurement Complexity

Procurement is moving at breakneck speed, but with transformation comes a surge in complexity-and staff are feeling the strain. Gartner’s latest research warns that cognitive overload is now a critical challenge for procurement teams, risking burnout, reduced effectiveness, and resistance to future change.

Cognitive Overload Is Real: Nearly half of procurement leaders have expanded their teams’ responsibilities recently, and most plan to add more. This “additive” approach, coupled with a volatile external environment, is overwhelming staff and stalling productivity-even as technology and education advance.

Redefine Productivity: The old model of measuring output per hour no longer fits. Leaders need to focus on increasing available cognition, not just available time. Automating simple tasks only works if the new, more complex tasks don’t drown staff in information and decision fatigue.

Design Simplicity Into Operations: To offset complexity, leaders should:

  • Develop clear templates, process maps, and decision rules for all procurement activities.
  • Separate tactical from strategic work to reduce mental switching costs.
  • Monitor user experience-not just engagement-by tracking workflow usability, complaints, and error rates

BCG AI

This article, in a similar way to the other reports on AI this month, highlights how generative AI is transforming procurement from a cost centre into a strategic value driver. AI enables procurement teams to achieve cost reductions of 15% to 45% across categories and can automate up to 30% of repetitive tasks, freeing staff to focus on more strategic work.

By rapidly analysing vast procurement data, AI identifies savings opportunities, enhances negotiation leverage, and accelerates processes like supplier communication and tender management. This not only boosts productivity but also helps mitigate operational and supply chain risks by providing real-time risk insights. Importantly, as suppliers themselves adopt AI, procurement teams must act fast to capture value and maintain a competitive edge. Success hinges on upskilling teams for new AI-powered workflows and embedding these tools into daily operations.

KPMG Pulse Survey

The latest KPMG Q1 2025 AI Pulse Survey reveals a surge in AI investment and adoption among large U.S. organizations, with leaders planning to boost GenAI spending to nearly $114 million over the next year. While daily use of AI productivity tools and knowledge assistants has jumped sharply, actual deployment of AI agents remains limited. Risk management, data quality, and trust in AI outputs are top concerns, with 82% of leaders citing risk as their biggest challenge.

Workforce readiness is also a sticking point, as complexity and skills gaps hinder broader implementation. Trust emerges as a critical issue, both within organizations and society at large, as leaders worry about accuracy, fairness, and misuse of AI. Notably, responsibility for AI initiatives is shifting from CEOs to CIOs, reflecting a move from strategy to operational execution. Overall, optimism about AI’s strategic value is high, but so is caution around risk and trust.

Beroe Tariffs

This article described how US tariff hikes in 2025-like the 145% tariff on China and 25% duties on Canada, Mexico, and key sectors-are sending shockwaves through global procurement. China’s 125% retaliatory tariffs and similar moves from other nations are reshaping trade routes, supplier dynamics, and cost structures.

Procurement leaders now face higher import costs, production delays from nearshoring, and increased risk of recession, with export-heavy economies and industries like food, pharma, electronics, and packaging feeling the pinch.

To stay resilient, procurement teams should:

  • Diversify sourcing to regions with lower tariffs.
  • Build regional resilience through joint ventures and improved logistics.
  • Negotiate flexible contracts to manage price volatility.

 

As always reach out to discuss more, and always happy to hear your thoughts!

entry.title: Approval Strategy for Sales Order
entry.content:
Dear SCN's

I need your help. The customer has a requirement: if a sales order has a discount of 9% or less, a 1-step approval strategy should be applied to the order. If a sales order has a discount of more than 9%, a 2-step approval strategy should be applied to the order. Is it possible to implement a Sales Order approval strategy according to this requirement and how?

 

SD (Sales and Distribution) SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: Account 64004000 requires an account assignment relev...
entry.content:
Hi Jeremy,

Please also check if you have configured "Account Assignment Objects" Cost center for Depreciation postings. ( reference - ASCET in GUI).

Let me know if this helps. 

 

Regards

Vijay

entry.title: Re: Account 64004000 requires an account assignment relev...
entry.content:
Hi Jeremy,

Cost center should be defaulted from the asset master. Please check the cost center validity date and also see there is no transactional lock on cost centers. If the issue still persist, raise an incident to SAP.

 

Regards

Satish

entry.title: Re: New SDK behavior with Microsoft Visual Studio 2022 (Professional)
entry.content: There is also a problem that the output window doesn't show logs while debugging is active as in the previous studio version.

entry.title: Re: Account 64004000 requires an account assignment relev...
entry.content: Please check your Fixed Asset master and see cost center is mentioned in the Fixed Asset. Then check Cost center validity and transaction lock on cost center master data

entry.title: Re: Period is open in MM but still is not allowing to pos...
entry.content:
Hi Vijay,

Please check your fiscal year - whether is calendar or different. You have to open MM period as per your fiscal year.

Example - If your fiscal year is April - March, Period 5 and 6 would be August and September.

Please check and let me know if it worked. If resolved, please mark it as resolved

 

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: PREP_GENCHECKS/EU_NAMETAB_CHECKERORR during upgrade from 1809 to 2023
entry.content:
i got this error when try to upgrade form 1809 to 2023

"Severe error(s) occurred in phase PREP_GENCHECKS/EU_NAMETAB_CHECK!"
A2 EEXP 017 Table DDNTT will be exported joined with fields table.
A3EESTR 001 Error for '/TMF/V_CC405_001': Field count 14 differs from header field count 0
A3EEEXP 008 Task failed with error: (EXP) Found 1 problems in data
A3EEEXP 008 (EXP) Table name is 'DDNTT'

can somebody help with this error?

entry.content:
1. We have created Datasource and Business rule with SOD Sub scenario for User level SOD report output.
  2. In Business rule we have maintained below details for Locked, Expired and Risk Analysis Report View.
    Expired User: Yes
    Locked User: Yes
    Risk Analysis Report Format: 3 ( We have also maintained numerical 1 and 2 to test but it didnt work)
  3. None of the above options seems to be working, We are not getting report in Business view nor Locked or Expired users are getting fetched

Incidentally, a lot of items seem to have disappeared from the Build Apps product roadmap.

Hope we get some clarification,

Thanks

Alessandro

entry.title: Re: Deprecated class de.hybris.platform.webservicescommon...
entry.content:
Yes, you are right. The sanitize() method in YSanitizer is different from the one in the Sanitizer class, but it seems that the OOTB code is using Sanitizer.sanitize() as a replacement. You can see the following classes as examples from the commercewebservices extension:

  • DefaultContextInformationLoader class under de.hybris.platform.commercewebservices.core.context.impl package
  • BaseSiteMatchingFilter class under de.hybris.platform.commercewebservices.core.v2.filter package
  • CartsController class under de.hybris.platform.commercewebservices.core.v2.controller package

First line of both methods are the same

StringUtils.defaultString(input).trim()

 After this line, YSanitizer class first removes all CRLF chars with space

CRLF.matcher(output).replaceAll(" ")

And after that calls escapeHtml method of org.apache.commons.lang.StringEscapeUtils. This method replaces some special chars with their HTML names (e.g. € becomes €)

StringEscapeUtils.escapeHtml(output)

However, after first line, Sanitizer class only removes the chars between 0-31 and 127-159 with the following line

CharMatcher.javaIsoControl().removeFrom(trimmed)

Since it removes the chars in the range from the string, CRLF chars (10 and 13) are also removed.

I wrote a simple Groovy to see the differences between to sanitize methods, you can also test it on HAC and see the differences:

import de.hybris.platform.util.Sanitizer
import de.hybris.platform.util.Utilities
import de.hybris.platform.webservicescommons.util.YSanitizer


StringBuilder chars = new StringBuilder();
for(char i = 0; i < 200; i++){
    chars.append("Char #");
    chars.append((int) i);
    chars.append(": ");
    chars.append(i);
    chars.append(System.lineSeparator());
}

String charsStr = chars.toString();
String ySanitized = YSanitizer.sanitize(charsStr);
String sanitized = Sanitizer.sanitize(charsStr);
String apacheLangHtmlEscapedAfterSanitized = org.apache.commons.lang.StringEscapeUtils.escapeHtml(sanitized);
String apacheTextHtmlEscaped3AfterSanitized = org.apache.commons.text.StringEscapeUtils.escapeHtml3(sanitized);
String apacheTextHtmlEscaped4AfterSanitized = org.apache.commons.text.StringEscapeUtils.escapeHtml4(sanitized);
String utilitiesHtmlEscapedAfterSanitized = Utilities.escapeHTML(sanitized);

println(charsStr);
println("YSanitizer.sanitize(): " + ySanitized)
println("Sanitizer.sanitize(): " + sanitized)
println("lang.StringEscapeUtils.escapeHtml(): " + apacheLangHtmlEscapedAfterSanitized)
println("text.StringEscapeUtils.escapeHtml3(): " + apacheTextHtmlEscaped3AfterSanitized)
println("text.StringEscapeUtils.escapeHtml4(): " + apacheTextHtmlEscaped4AfterSanitized)
println("Utilities.escapeHTML(): " + utilitiesHtmlEscapedAfterSanitized)

 Hope this clarifies the difference between two classes

entry.title: Re: Upload account statement MT940 incorrect
entry.content:
Hi Stanco,

28C is a Bank statement number and It seems your MT940 file has multiple bank statements in single file. You can upload multiple bank statements in single MT940 file but make sure 28C is different for each line. 

Please check and upload, it should work.

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: How sovanta uses Joule Studio to take ticketing systems to the next level
entry.content:
Real-World Use Case with SAP Joule Studio

In an era where efficiency and intelligent automation are critical, SAP has introduced Joule – a powerful AI assistant that is redefining the way users interact with SAP systems. But what do real-world use cases look like when it comes to optimizing business processes and supporting employees across departments? As an early-stage adopter of Joule Studio, sovanta put the GenAI tool through its paces. From Extension to Integration to AI – sovanta has been consulting and supporting SAP customers in the cloud environment for 15 years and has developed into a specialized SAP BTP partner. Thanks to our experienced BTP, UX, AI, and Development experts, we have established ourselves as a reliable BTP implementation partner, focusing on innovative, easy-to-use and AI-powered business apps that semelssly integrate with various systems.

Bringing together our SAP and AI experience, we successfully implemented the first use case with Joule Studio. The result? An exciting use case in the Center domain. In this blog post, we’ll explore the background of that use case and how our clients can benefit from Joule going forward.

Joule Studio in SAP Build: What It Can Do

First things first: We took a deep dive into Joule Studio within SAP Build, and we’re excited by what we found. The combination of generative AI and deep integration into SAP systems already makes Joule a powerful tool for our clients. But the real magic happens with the addition of Custom Joule Skills via Joule Studio. These enable truly impactful use cases that can make a tangible difference for businesses.

A particularly interesting aspect – and challenge – is Joule’s chat history. Joule automatically logs conversations to autonomously select the appropriate skill for follow-up questions. However, it heavily depends on the descriptions of each skill to correctly trigger them. As Joule Studio pioneer, we observed that Joule occasionally struggles to trigger the most relevant skill for certain follow-ups. By fine-tuning our skill descriptions, we were able to significantly improve precision and context handling. We are excited to see that SAP is actively working to enhance Skill Matching capabilities, and partner feedback like ours is directly shaping the ongoing evolution of Joule Studio’s intelligence.

The Use Case: Ticketing System Integration

To fully explore Joule Studio’s potential, we developed a realistic use case focused on service centers. This scenario showcases how Joule can retrieve service case information by integrating with an external ticketing system and leveraging Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) for contextual insights. This allows users to instantly access relevant ticket data and receive solution recommendations based on similar past cases – even without relying on Joule’s Document Grounding capabilities, which were not yet available during our early adopter experience.

The Business Challenge

When a customer reports an issue with a product, service teams are confronted with a lot of challenges:

  • Understanding the specifics of the incoming service case, extracting key information from it
  • Quickly finding internal documents that may be relevant to the customer issue
  • Checking if a similar issue was previously reported or resolved

All of these actions require the service team to navigate between various different systems, having to copy and paste information and search terms across these.

The Solution and its Key Benefits

This is where the power of Joule Studio and its ability to connect Joule with various systems comes in handy. Making full use of custom skills, we connected:

  • A ticketing system (e.g., JIRA) via API to retrieve and update service tickets.
  • The sovanta GenAI DocumentChat via API to perform RAG using historical case data.
    Good to know: While native RAG orchestration via Document Grounding is still evolving, Joule Studio already makes it easy to integrate external RAG services through APIs as part of a custom Joule Skill. In our case, we demonstrated how external RAG can be connected— using the sovanta GenAI DocumentChat to bring additional context and insights into the conversation.

We allow Joule to interact with these systems via three separate Joule Skills:

  • Skill 1: Search Tickets: Allows Joule to search for specific Tickets in the ticketing System.
  • Skill 2: Find Past Solutions: Use RAG via a context in the Document Chat that contains past solutions.
  • Skill 3: Update Ticket: Allows Joule to update a specific ticket in the ticketing System.

The flow below shows how these skills work together in a real conversation.

Separating the new Joule skills helps make each step in the process more reliable and easier to control. By keeping actions like searching tickets, finding solutions, and updating cases separate, we make sure Joule picks the right skill depending on the user’s request. This approach also ties closely to the importance of good skill descriptions: when each skill is focused and clearly described, Joule can match follow-up questions much more accurately. It keeps the conversation flow smooth and gives us the flexibility to tweak or improve individual skills later without disrupting the whole setup.

This use case shows how a custom Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) service can already be effectively integrated with Joule Studio, even without native support. As a result, support teams benefit from context-aware, AI-generated responses that boost efficiency and improve the overall customer service experience. By instantly retrieving and analyzing ticket data, Joule enables significantly faster issue resolution. It also supports proactive problem-solving by suggesting solutions based on similar past cases.

Future of AI Agents in Joule Studio

Looking ahead, building AI Agents within Joule Studio will be a key next step. As participants in the SAP Alpha Program for AI Agents, we already see strong potential to extend our use case even further. In the future, AI Agents could completely take over first-level support activities such as autonomously requesting additional information, suggesting possible solutions and reacting to further feedback by customers.

This will also require handling ticket escalations efficiently. In many service centers, one common challenge is identifying when a support ticket needs to be escalated. Service agents often have to manually decide if an issue is urgent enough to involve higher-level support, which can lead to delays or missed escalations. With large ticket volumes, it’s easy for critical issues to get lost among routine cases. AI Agents in Joule Studio could help by proactively monitoring tickets for escalation triggers and forwarding those tickets to the correct second- or third level support experts — taking service center efficiency to the next level. 

Our Final Thoughts on Joule Studio

Joule Studio is proving to be a promising addition to the SAP ecosystem. With the ability to extend capabilities using Custom Skills and integrate external services like sovanta GenAI DocumentChat, it offers a flexible and future-proof way to enhance business processes. Integrating Joule with AI Agents will open up exciting possibilities for realizing advanced use cases within Joule Studio, taking full advantage of multi-skill orchestration and autonomous task planning and handling. At sovanta, we’re excited to continue exploring how our clients can benefit from SAP Joule – today and in the future.

entry.title: Time Forms
entry.content:
Is it possible to create a absence statement with a column of worklfow approver and workflow status. The requirement is that an employee has to hand over a form with the absences to a union before going on holiday etc. I immedately thought of time forms since reports can also cover this topic but the customer asked specific about this option.
If it is possible how has the template and the mapping look like?

entry.title: Re: Release SiteLogisticsRequest with OData in ByD
entry.content: Hello. Can you show your payload please?

entry.title: "IS ASSIGNED" doesn't work after dynamic ASSIGN (in a loop)
entry.content:
I have the below piece of code, I expect that the field symbol is not assigned for the component ATRIBUT (the component doesn't exist), but it says it's assigned, with the value of the previous component ID:

REPORT zdemo.
CLASS ltc_is_assigned_wrong_usage DEFINITION FINAL
  FOR TESTING RISK LEVEL HARMLESS DURATION SHORT.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS test FOR TESTING RAISING cx_static_check.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS ltc_is_assigned_wrong_usage IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD test.
    TYPES:
      BEGIN OF ty_object,
        id        TYPE c LENGTH 10,
        attribute TYPE c LENGTH 10,
      END OF ty_object.
    DATA(object) = VALUE ty_object( id        = 'Value-ID'
                                    attribute = 'Value-Attr' ).
    DATA(component_names) = VALUE string_table( ( `WRONG` ) ( `ID` ) ( `ATRIBUT` ) ( `ATTRIBUTE` ) ).
    DATA(result) = VALUE string( ).
    LOOP AT component_names INTO DATA(component_name).
      ASSIGN COMPONENT component_name OF STRUCTURE object TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<component>).
      IF <component> IS ASSIGNED. "<===== doesn't work well ???
        result = result && |{ component_name } value: { <component> }\n|.
      ELSE.
        result = result && |{ component_name } not assigned\n|.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals( 
          act = result
          exp = |WRONG not assigned\n|
             && |ID value: Value-ID\n|
             && |ATRIBUT value: Value-ID\n| "<==== I expect ATRIBUT not assigned
             && |ATTRIBUTE value: Value-Attr\n| ).
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

Why does "IS ASSIGNED" return true for the non-existing component name ATRIBUT?

Thank you.

Sandra
